#libs
```{r}
library(phyloseq)
library(microbiome)
library(pheatmap)
library(grid)
library(gtable)
```

# load data and transform
```{r}
#Read phyloseq
ps <-  readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v2.rds')
ps_obj <- ps


# ==============================================================================
# Filter specimens
print('After specimen selection:')
ps_obj
# ==============================================================================
# Filter taxa
prevalence <- 10/100
detection <- 1/100
taxas <- core_members(ps_obj, detection = detection, prevalence = prevalence, include.lowest = F)
ps_obj <- prune_taxa(taxas, ps_obj)

print('After taxa filtering:')
ps_obj


# ==============================================================================
# Agglomeration
tax_glom_level <- 'Species'
ps_obj <- speedyseq::tax_glom(ps_obj, tax_glom_level, NArm =T)
print('After agglomeration:')
ps_obj

transformation <- 'clr'
ps_obj <- microbiome::transform(ps_obj, transformation)

prepare_count_table_simple <- function(ps_obj) {
  # Get the OTU table (counts)
  otu_matrix <- as(otu_table(ps_obj), 'matrix')
  
  # Transpose if taxa are rows (make samples as rows, taxa as columns)
  if (taxa_are_rows(ps_obj)) {
    otu_matrix <- t(otu_matrix)
  }
  
  # Get taxonomy table
  taxa_matrix <- as(tax_table(ps_obj), 'matrix')
  
  # Create simple names: Family.Genus.Species (with NA if missing)
  simple_names <- apply(taxa_matrix, 1, function(x) {
    # Extract family, genus, species (will be NA if missing)
    #family <- ifelse(is.na(x['Family']), "NA", x['Family'])
    #genus <- ifelse(is.na(x['Genus']), "NA", x['Genus'])
    species <- ifelse(is.na(x['Species']), "NA", x['Species'])
    
    # Remove problematic characters
    #family <- gsub('/', '', gsub("-", "", family))
    #genus <- gsub('/', '', gsub("-", "", genus))
    species <- gsub('/', '', gsub("-", "", species))
    
    # Combine with underscores
    #paste(family, genus, species, sep = "_")
    paste(species, sep = "_")
  })
  
  # Set column names
  colnames(otu_matrix) <- simple_names
  
  return(otu_matrix)
}

# Run the function
count_mtrx <- prepare_count_table_simple(ps_obj)

# View the result
head(count_mtrx[, 1:5])  # First 5 columns
dim(count_mtrx)  # Dimensions (samples x taxa)

# ==============================================================================
# Transform counts


taxa_name_func <- function(x) {paste0(x['ASV'],  '_' , gsub('/', '', gsub("-", "", x['Species'])))}
# ==============================================================================
# Prepare metadata table
ps_meta <- as(sample_data(ps_obj), 'data.frame')
categorical_vars <- names(ps_meta)[sapply(ps_meta, function(x) is.factor(x) || is.character(x))]

# Remove 'timepoint' from the list if it exists
# Remove 'Group' from categorical_vars since that's our grouping variable
categorical_vars <- setdiff(categorical_vars, c('Age',"Group", 'Height', 'No_Patient','Weight','Timepoint','SampleID'))
#cols_of_interest =c('Antibiotics_treatment_in_last_6_months','Treatment_of_periodontitis', 'Sex', 'Age_group', 'BMI_group', 'Stage_of_severity_of_periodontitis', 'Smoking_status','Arterial hypertension')
meta_for_heatmap <- ps_meta[, (names(ps_meta) %in% categorical_vars)]
for (col in categorical_vars) {
  if (col %in% colnames(ps_meta) && (is.character(ps_meta[[col]]) || is.integer(ps_meta[[col]]))) {
    ps_obj@sam_data[[col]] <- as.factor(ps_obj@sam_data[[col]])
  }
}
count_mtrx <- prepare_count_table_simple(ps_obj)
ordered_counts <-count_mtrx[rownames(meta_for_heatmap),]
taxa_var <- apply(count_mtrx, 2, var, na.rm = TRUE)
top_taxa <- names(sort(taxa_var, decreasing = TRUE)[1:100])  # keep top 50
count_mtrx <- count_mtrx[, top_taxa, drop = FALSE]

# ==============================================================================
# Plot Heatmap
clustering_distance_rows <- 'euclidean'
# clustering_distance_rows <- 'binary'
# clustering_distance_rows <- 'correlation'

clustering_distance_cols <- 'euclidean'
# clustering_distance_cols <- 'binary'
# clustering_distance_cols <- 'correlation'

save_path <- '/home/sonec_iv/'
filename <- paste0(save_path, 'RSF_WGS_MOD3_tax_glom_level_', tax_glom_level, '_transform_', transformation, '_det', detection, '_prev',prevalence,  '_clust_dist_cols_', clustering_distance_cols,  '_clust_dist_rows_', clustering_distance_rows, '.pdf')
```

# Heatmap ORIG
```{r}
p <-pheatmap(t(count_mtrx),
             cluster_rows = T, treeheight_row  = 90, treeheight_col = 90,
             cluster_cols = T,
             fontsize=48,
             annotation_col = meta_for_heatmap,
             silent=F,
             clustering_distance_rows=clustering_distance_rows,
             clustering_distance_cols=clustering_distance_cols,
             clustering_method='ward.D2',
             gaps_col = breaks,
             fontsize_row=48,
             fontsize_col=48,
             show_rownames = T,
             show_colnames = T,
             border_color=NA,
             width=140, height = 110,
             filename = filename)
p
```


# heatmap MOD
```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# Full Heatmap Script with Custom Sample Order, Colored Labels, Enlarged Legend
# and Elongated Colorbar
# ==============================================================================
# --- 1. Prepare custom column order: sort by base ID then suffix ---
sample_names <- rownames(count_mtrx)                     # samples as rows
base_id <- gsub("-[12]$", "", sample_names)              # remove trailing -1/-2
suffix <- gsub(".*-", "", sample_names)
order_df <- data.frame(sample = sample_names,
                       base   = base_id,
                       suffix = suffix)
order_df$suffix_num <- as.numeric(suffix)                # numeric for proper sort
order_df <- order_df[order(order_df$base, order_df$suffix_num), ]
sorted_samples <- order_df$sample

# --- 2. Reorder matrices accordingly ---
count_mtrx_ordered <- count_mtrx[sorted_samples, ]
meta_for_heatmap_ordered <- meta_for_heatmap[sorted_samples, , drop = FALSE]

# --- 3. Column label colours (blue for -1, red for -2) ---
col_label_colors <- ifelse(grepl("-1", sorted_samples), "blue",
                           ifelse(grepl("-2", sorted_samples), "red", "black"))

# --- 4. Create pheatmap object (no auto‑save) ---
p <- pheatmap(t(count_mtrx_ordered),                     # transpose: samples as columns
             cluster_rows = TRUE,
             cluster_cols = FALSE,
             order_cols = sorted_samples,                # use our custom order
             treeheight_row = 90,
             treeheight_col = 0,                          # no column dendrogram
             fontsize = 32,                               # base font size
             annotation_col = meta_for_heatmap_ordered,
             silent = TRUE,
             clustering_distance_rows = clustering_distance_rows,
             clustering_method = 'ward.D2',
             # gaps_col = breaks,                         # optional, if defined
             fontsize_row = 32,
             fontsize_col = 32,
             show_rownames = TRUE,
             show_colnames = TRUE,
             border_color = "white",                      # cell spacing
             width = 140, height = 110,
             filename = NA)

# --- 5. Colour column labels (using layout table) ---
col_names_idx <- which(p$gtable$layout$name == "col_names")
if (length(col_names_idx) == 1) {
  col_grob <- p$gtable$grobs[[col_names_idx]]
  if (inherits(col_grob, "text")) {
    col_grob$gp$col <- col_label_colors
    p$gtable$grobs[[col_names_idx]] <- col_grob
  }
}

# --- 6. Increase legend text size to 48 ---
legend_idx <- which(p$gtable$layout$name %in% c("legend", "guide-box"))
for (idx in legend_idx) {
  leg_grob <- p$gtable$grobs[[idx]]
  
  # Recursively find all text grobs inside the legend
  if (inherits(leg_grob, "gTree")) {
    text_children <- which(sapply(leg_grob$children,
                                  function(child) inherits(child, "text")))
    for (j in text_children) {
      leg_grob$children[[j]]$gp$fontsize <- 32
    }
  } else if (inherits(leg_grob, "text")) {
    leg_grob$gp$fontsize <- 32
  }
  
  p$gtable$grobs[[idx]] <- leg_grob
}

# --- 7. Move the legend 1 inch left and 0.5 inch down ---
for (idx in legend_idx) {
  leg_grob <- p$gtable$grobs[[idx]]
  
  if (is.null(leg_grob$vp)) {
    leg_grob$vp <- viewport(x = 0.5, y = 0.5, just = c("center", "center"))
  }
  leg_grob$vp$x <- unit(0.5, "npc") + unit(-1, "inches")   # left
  leg_grob$vp$y <- unit(0.5, "npc") + unit(-0.5, "inches") # down
  leg_grob$vp$just <- c("center", "center")
  
  p$gtable$grobs[[idx]] <- leg_grob
}

# --- 8. Lengthen the colorbar inside the legend (double its height) ---
for (idx in legend_idx) {
  leg_grob <- p$gtable$grobs[[idx]]
  
  if (inherits(leg_grob, "gTree")) {
    # Find all raster grobs (the colorbar is a rasterGrob)
    raster_children <- which(sapply(leg_grob$children,
                                    function(child) inherits(child, "rastergrob")))
    for (r in raster_children) {
      # Double the height of the raster
      if (!is.null(leg_grob$children[[r]]$height)) {
        leg_grob$children[[r]]$height <- leg_grob$children[[r]]$height * 6
      } else {
        # If height not set, assign a default and double
        leg_grob$children[[r]]$height <- unit(1, "inches") * 6
      }
      # Ensure the raster is still centered
      leg_grob$children[[r]]$y <- unit(0.5, "npc")
      leg_grob$children[[r]]$just <- "center"
    }
  }
  
  p$gtable$grobs[[idx]] <- leg_grob
}

# --- 9. Save and display ---
pdf(file = filename, width = 140, height = 110)
grid.draw(p$gtable)
dev.off()

grid.newpage()
grid.draw(p$gtable)
```

# MOD3
```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# Pheatmap with Custom Order, Colored Labels, Adjusted Legend, and More Row Spacing
# ==============================================================================

# --- 1. Prepare custom column order: sort by base ID then suffix ---
sample_names <- rownames(count_mtrx)
base_id <- gsub("-[12]$", "", sample_names)
suffix <- gsub(".*-", "", sample_names)
order_df <- data.frame(sample = sample_names,
                       base   = base_id,
                       suffix = suffix)
order_df$suffix_num <- as.numeric(suffix)
order_df <- order_df[order(order_df$base, order_df$suffix_num), ]
sorted_samples <- order_df$sample

# --- 2. Reorder matrices ---
count_mtrx_ordered <- count_mtrx[sorted_samples, ]
meta_for_heatmap_ordered <- meta_for_heatmap[sorted_samples, , drop = FALSE]

# --- 3. Column label colours (blue for -1, red for -2) ---
col_label_colors <- ifelse(grepl("-1", sorted_samples), "blue",
                           ifelse(grepl("-2", sorted_samples), "red", "black"))

# --- 4. Create pheatmap object with increased height, smaller row font, and fewer legend breaks ---
p <- pheatmap(t(count_mtrx_ordered),
             cluster_rows = TRUE,
             cluster_cols = FALSE,
             order_cols = sorted_samples,
             treeheight_row = 180,
             treeheight_col = 0,
             fontsize = 32,                          # base font size
             annotation_col = meta_for_heatmap_ordered,
             silent = TRUE,
             clustering_distance_rows = clustering_distance_rows,
             clustering_method = 'ward.D2',
             # gaps_col = breaks,                     # optional
             fontsize_row = 28,                       # smaller row labels to reduce overlap
             fontsize_col = 32,
             show_rownames = TRUE,
             show_colnames = TRUE,
             border_color = "white",                  # cell borders for spacing
             #legend_breaks = seq(-4, 10, by = 3),     # fewer breaks -> less crowding
             width = 140, height = 290,                # taller plot → more row spacing
             filename = NA)

# --- 5. Colour column labels (using layout table) ---
col_names_idx <- which(p$gtable$layout$name == "col_names")
if (length(col_names_idx) == 1) {
  col_grob <- p$gtable$grobs[[col_names_idx]]
  if (inherits(col_grob, "text")) {
    col_grob$gp$col <- col_label_colors
    p$gtable$grobs[[col_names_idx]] <- col_grob
  }
}

# --- 6. Increase legend text size to 40 (safe value) ---
legend_idx <- which(p$gtable$layout$name %in% c("legend", "guide-box"))
for (idx in legend_idx) {
  leg_grob <- p$gtable$grobs[[idx]]
  
  if (inherits(leg_grob, "gTree")) {
    text_children <- which(sapply(leg_grob$children,
                                  function(child) inherits(child, "text")))
    for (j in text_children) {
      leg_grob$children[[j]]$gp$fontsize <- 32
    }
  } else if (inherits(leg_grob, "text")) {
    leg_grob$gp$fontsize <- 32
  }
  
  p$gtable$grobs[[idx]] <- leg_grob
}

# --- 7. Move the legend 1 inch left and 0.5 inch down ---
for (idx in legend_idx) {
  leg_grob <- p$gtable$grobs[[idx]]
  
  if (is.null(leg_grob$vp)) {
    leg_grob$vp <- viewport(x = 0.5, y = 0.5, just = c("center", "center"))
  }
  leg_grob$vp$x <- unit(0.5, "npc") + unit(0, "inches")   # left
  leg_grob$vp$y <- unit(0.5, "npc") + unit(-55, "inches") # down
  leg_grob$vp$just <- c("center", "center")
  
  p$gtable$grobs[[idx]] <- leg_grob
}

# --- 8. Lengthen the colorbar inside the legend (double its height) ---
for (idx in legend_idx) {
  leg_grob <- p$gtable$grobs[[idx]]
  
  if (inherits(leg_grob, "gTree")) {
    # Find all raster grobs (the colorbar is a rasterGrob)
    raster_children <- which(sapply(leg_grob$children,
                                    function(child) inherits(child, "rastergrob")))
    for (r in raster_children) {
      # Double the height of the raster
      if (!is.null(leg_grob$children[[r]]$height)) {
        leg_grob$children[[r]]$height <- leg_grob$children[[r]]$height * 5
      } else {
        # If height not set, assign a default and double
        leg_grob$children[[r]]$height <- unit(1, "inches") * 5
      }
      # Ensure the raster is still centered
      leg_grob$children[[r]]$y <- unit(0.5, "npc")
      leg_grob$children[[r]]$just <- "center"
    }
  }
  
  p$gtable$grobs[[idx]] <- leg_grob
}

# --- 9. Save and display ---
pdf(file = filename, width = 140, height = 290)
grid.draw(p$gtable)
dev.off()

grid.newpage()
grid.draw(p$gtable)
```

# mod3 top100 taxa
```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# Pheatmap with Custom Order, Colored Labels, Adjusted Legend,
# and Reduced Number of Taxa for Better Readability
# ==============================================================================
filename <- paste0(save_path, 'RSF_WGS_MOD3_top100_tax_glom_level_', tax_glom_level, '_transform_', transformation, '_det', detection, '_prev',prevalence,  '_clust_dist_cols_', clustering_distance_cols,  '_clust_dist_rows_', clustering_distance_rows, '.pdf')

# --- 1. Prepare custom column order: sort by base ID then suffix ---
sample_names <- rownames(count_mtrx)
base_id <- gsub("-[12]$", "", sample_names)
suffix <- gsub(".*-", "", sample_names)
order_df <- data.frame(sample = sample_names,
                       base   = base_id,
                       suffix = suffix)
order_df$suffix_num <- as.numeric(suffix)
order_df <- order_df[order(order_df$base, order_df$suffix_num), ]
sorted_samples <- order_df$sample

# --- 2. Reorder matrices ---
count_mtrx_ordered <- count_mtrx[sorted_samples, ]
meta_for_heatmap_ordered <- meta_for_heatmap[sorted_samples, , drop = FALSE]

# --- 3. Filter to top 50 most variable taxa (adjust number as needed) ---
taxa_var <- apply(count_mtrx_ordered, 2, var, na.rm = TRUE)
top_taxa <- names(sort(taxa_var, decreasing = TRUE)[1:100])  # keep top 50
count_mtrx_filtered <- count_mtrx_ordered[, top_taxa, drop = FALSE]

# --- 4. Column label colours (blue for -1, red for -2) ---
col_label_colors <- ifelse(grepl("-1", sorted_samples), "blue",
                           ifelse(grepl("-2", sorted_samples), "red", "black"))

# --- 5. Create pheatmap object with filtered data ---
p <- pheatmap(t(count_mtrx_filtered),                     # transpose: samples as columns
             cluster_rows = TRUE,
             cluster_cols = FALSE,
             order_cols = sorted_samples,
             treeheight_row = 90,
             treeheight_col = 0,
             fontsize = 32,                                # base font size
             annotation_col = meta_for_heatmap_ordered,
             silent = TRUE,
             clustering_distance_rows = clustering_distance_rows,
             clustering_method = 'ward.D2',
             # gaps_col = breaks,                          # optional
             fontsize_row = 32,                             # moderate row label size
             fontsize_col = 32,
             show_rownames = TRUE,
             show_colnames = TRUE,
             border_color = "white",                        # cell borders for spacing
             legend_breaks = seq(-4, 10, by = 3),           # fewer breaks → less crowding
             width = 140, height = 110,                     # reasonable height
             filename = NA)

# --- 6. Colour column labels ---
col_names_idx <- which(p$gtable$layout$name == "col_names")
if (length(col_names_idx) == 1) {
  col_grob <- p$gtable$grobs[[col_names_idx]]
  if (inherits(col_grob, "text")) {
    col_grob$gp$col <- col_label_colors
    p$gtable$grobs[[col_names_idx]] <- col_grob
  }
}

# --- 7. Increase legend text size to 40 ---
legend_idx <- which(p$gtable$layout$name %in% c("legend", "guide-box"))
for (idx in legend_idx) {
  leg_grob <- p$gtable$grobs[[idx]]
  
  if (inherits(leg_grob, "gTree")) {
    text_children <- which(sapply(leg_grob$children,
                                  function(child) inherits(child, "text")))
    for (j in text_children) {
      leg_grob$children[[j]]$gp$fontsize <- 40
    }
  } else if (inherits(leg_grob, "text")) {
    leg_grob$gp$fontsize <- 40
  }
  
  p$gtable$grobs[[idx]] <- leg_grob
}

# --- 8. Move the legend 1 inch left and 0.5 inch down ---
for (idx in legend_idx) {
  leg_grob <- p$gtable$grobs[[idx]]
  
  if (is.null(leg_grob$vp)) {
    leg_grob$vp <- viewport(x = 0.5, y = 0.5, just = c("center", "center"))
  }
  leg_grob$vp$x <- unit(0.5, "npc") + unit(-1, "inches")   # left
  leg_grob$vp$y <- unit(0.5, "npc") + unit(-0.5, "inches") # down
  leg_grob$vp$just <- c("center", "center")
  
  p$gtable$grobs[[idx]] <- leg_grob
}

# --- 9. Save and display ---
pdf(file = filename, width = 140, height = 110)
grid.draw(p$gtable)
dev.off()

grid.newpage()
grid.draw(p$gtable)
```

# mod3 top100 rare taxa & grouped by Timepoint or SampleID
```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# Two Heatmaps: Timepoint-Ordered (Top 100 Variable) and Least Abundant Species
# ==============================================================================

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 0. Common preparations
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Original paired sample order (as before)
sample_names <- rownames(count_mtrx)
base_id <- gsub("-[12]$", "", sample_names)
suffix <- gsub(".*-", "", sample_names)
order_df <- data.frame(sample = sample_names,
                       base   = base_id,
                       suffix = suffix)
order_df$suffix_num <- as.numeric(suffix)
order_df <- order_df[order(order_df$base, order_df$suffix_num), ]
sorted_samples_paired <- order_df$sample

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Timepoint‑ordered sample vector (all -1 first, then -2)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Extract suffix and sort by it, then by base ID to keep within-group order consistent
timepoint_df <- data.frame(sample = sample_names,
                           base = base_id,
                           suffix = suffix)
timepoint_df <- timepoint_df[order(timepoint_df$suffix, timepoint_df$base), ]
sorted_samples_timepoint <- timepoint_df$sample

# Colors for timepoint order
#col_label_colors_timepoint <- ifelse(grepl("-1", sorted_samples_timepoint), "blue",
#                                     ifelse(grepl("-2", sorted_samples_timepoint), "red", "black"))

col_label_colors_timepoint <- ifelse(endsWith(sorted_samples_timepoint, "-1"), "blue",
                                     ifelse(endsWith(sorted_samples_timepoint, "-2"), "red", "black"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Select top 100 most variable species (for timepoint heatmap)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
taxa_var <- apply(count_mtrx, 2, var, na.rm = TRUE)          # across all samples
top100_var <- names(sort(taxa_var, decreasing = TRUE)[1:100])

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Select 100 least abundant species (by total sum across samples)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
taxa_sum <- colSums(count_mtrx, na.rm = TRUE)
least100_abund <- names(sort(taxa_sum, decreasing = FALSE)[1:100])   # smallest sums first

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Function to create a formatted heatmap with given parameters
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
create_heatmap <- function(count_matrix, sample_order, taxa_subset,
                           col_colors, filename_suffix) {
  
  # Reorder samples and subset taxa
  count_ordered <- count_matrix[sample_order, taxa_subset, drop = FALSE]
  meta_ordered <- meta_for_heatmap[sample_order, , drop = FALSE]
  
  # Build pheatmap object
  p <- pheatmap(t(count_ordered),
                cluster_rows = TRUE,
                cluster_cols = FALSE,
                order_cols = sample_order,
                treeheight_row = 90,
                treeheight_col = 0,
                fontsize = 32,
                annotation_col = meta_ordered,
                silent = TRUE,
                clustering_distance_rows = clustering_distance_rows,
                clustering_method = 'ward.D2',
                fontsize_row = 48,                # smaller row labels for readability
                fontsize_col = 36,
                show_rownames = TRUE,
                show_colnames = TRUE,
                border_color = "white",
                legend_breaks = seq(-4, 10, by = 3),
                width = 140, height = 140,
                filename = NA)
  
  # Colour column labels
  col_names_idx <- which(p$gtable$layout$name == "col_names")
  if (length(col_names_idx) == 1) {
    col_grob <- p$gtable$grobs[[col_names_idx]]
    if (inherits(col_grob, "text")) {
      col_grob$gp$col <- col_colors
      p$gtable$grobs[[col_names_idx]] <- col_grob
    }
  }
  
  # Increase legend text size to 40
  legend_idx <- which(p$gtable$layout$name %in% c("legend", "guide-box"))
  for (idx in legend_idx) {
    leg_grob <- p$gtable$grobs[[idx]]
    if (inherits(leg_grob, "gTree")) {
      text_children <- which(sapply(leg_grob$children, function(child) inherits(child, "text")))
      for (j in text_children) {
        leg_grob$children[[j]]$gp$fontsize <- 40
      }
    } else if (inherits(leg_grob, "text")) {
      leg_grob$gp$fontsize <- 40
    }
    p$gtable$grobs[[idx]] <- leg_grob
  }
  
  # Move legend 1 inch left, 0.5 inch down
  for (idx in legend_idx) {
    leg_grob <- p$gtable$grobs[[idx]]
    if (is.null(leg_grob$vp)) {
      leg_grob$vp <- viewport(x = 0.5, y = 0.5, just = c("center", "center"))
    }
    leg_grob$vp$x <- unit(0.5, "npc") + unit(-1, "inches")
    leg_grob$vp$y <- unit(0.5, "npc") + unit(-0.5, "inches")
    leg_grob$vp$just <- c("center", "center")
    p$gtable$grobs[[idx]] <- leg_grob
  }
  
  # Save
  filename <- paste0(save_path, 'RSF_WGS_', filename_suffix,
                     '_tax_glom_level_', tax_glom_level,
                     '_transform_', transformation, '_det', detection,
                     '_prev', prevalence, '.pdf')
  pdf(file = filename, width = 140, height = 140)
  grid.draw(p$gtable)
  dev.off()
  
  # Return the p object for optional display
  return(p)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Generate Heatmap A: Timepoint‑ordered, top 100 variable species
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
p_A <- create_heatmap(
  count_matrix = count_mtrx,
  sample_order = sorted_samples_timepoint,
  taxa_subset = top100_var,
  col_colors = col_label_colors_timepoint,
  filename_suffix = "timepointOrdered_top100var"
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Generate Heatmap B: Timepoint‑ordered, 100 least abundant species
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

p_B <- create_heatmap(
  count_matrix = count_mtrx,
  sample_order = sorted_samples_timepoint,
  taxa_subset = least100_abund,
  col_colors = col_label_colors_timepoint,
  filename_suffix = "timepointOrdered_least100var"
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7. Optionally display one of them in R
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Colors for paired order (same logic as timepoint but using sorted_samples_paired)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
col_label_colors_paired <- ifelse(endsWith(sorted_samples_paired, "-1"), "blue",
                                  ifelse(endsWith(sorted_samples_paired, "-2"), "red", "black"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Heatmap C: Paired-ordered, top 100 variable species
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
p_C <- create_heatmap(
  count_matrix = count_mtrx,
  sample_order = sorted_samples_paired,
  taxa_subset = top100_var,
  col_colors = col_label_colors_paired,
  filename_suffix = "pairedOrdered_top100var"
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Heatmap D: Paired-ordered, 100 least abundant species
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
p_D <- create_heatmap(
  count_matrix = count_mtrx,
  sample_order = sorted_samples_paired,
  taxa_subset = least100_abund,
  col_colors = col_label_colors_paired,
  filename_suffix = "pairedOrdered_least100abund"
)
```

#DEBUG for samples BO-101/103
```{r}
# After count_mtrx and meta_for_heatmap are defined
sample_names <- rownames(count_mtrx)
base_id <- gsub("-[12]$", "", sample_names)
suffix <- gsub(".*-", "", sample_names)

# Create a data frame for inspection
sample_info <- data.frame(
  sample = sample_names,
  base   = base_id,
  suffix = suffix,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Check problematic samples
print(sample_info[sample_info$sample %in% c("BO-101-1", "BO-101-2", "BO-103-1", "BO-103-2"), ])
# Build the timepoint order
timepoint_df <- sample_info[order(sample_info$suffix, sample_info$base), ]
sorted_samples_timepoint <- timepoint_df$sample

# Colors for this order
col_colors_timepoint <- ifelse(grepl("-1", sorted_samples_timepoint), "blue",
                               ifelse(grepl("-2", sorted_samples_timepoint), "red", "black"))

# Check where the problematic samples are and their assigned color
problem_idx <- which(sorted_samples_timepoint %in% c("BO-101-1", "BO-101-2", "BO-103-1", "BO-103-2"))
data.frame(
  sample = sorted_samples_timepoint[problem_idx],
  color  = col_colors_timepoint[problem_idx]
)
```
# DEBUG pt2
```{r}
# After building sample_info (from earlier)
prob_samples <- c("BO-101-1", "BO-101-2", "BO-103-1", "BO-103-2")

# Show raw character codes (hex)
for (s in prob_samples) {
  cat(s, ":", charToRaw(s), "\n")
}
```
